research.status_summary: Make (formerly Integromat) is actively operating and product-shipping as of June 2026, with no layoffs reported — self-described as 'profitable' and growing, with ~706 employees as of April 2026.
research.hiring_policy: Make's careers page advertises active hiring with RSU grants, flexible/remote working with a monthly remote allowance, and flexible hours, but no public mention of visa sponsorship or explicit EOR-friendly hiring; as a Czech-HQ'd EU company, international hiring likely follows European work-permit norms rather than EOR models.
recent_news: Next-generation Make AI Agents launched February 11, 2026 — fully integrated into the visual Scenario Builder with a real-time Reasoning Panel, available on all paid plans.

Maia, Make's conversational AI build assistant (describe a workflow in plain language, Maia wires the modules), is in early access as of May 2026 — not yet GA, with per-plan weekly message limits already documented.

Make Waves '26 announced for Prague, October 19–20, 2026 — Make's flagship annual conference moving to HQ city for the first time, targeting 700+ automation and AI builders; tickets went on sale April 23, 2026.
Make Grid (real-time auto-generated visual map of entire automation landscape) rolled out to all paid users in 2025 and is now a core platform pillar entering 2026.
Make announced official end-of-support dates for the legacy Integromat platform, completing the full brand/product migration.
research.growth_signal: Make reports being profitable with ~706 employees (April 2026, per Tracxn), powers 250,000–500,000+ organizations, and is on an accelerating product-release cadence (AI Agents, Maia, Grid, MCP) with a growing annual conference — trajectory is expansion, not contraction.
research.fit_for_lior: Make is a direct sweet spot: its entire 2026 product story — Maia (vibe-coding automations via natural language), AI Agents, and Make Grid — maps precisely to Lior's Creative AI, Vibe Coder, and Product Analyst threads, and the no-code-first DNA means PM/analyst roles here sit at the intersection of AI product strategy and workflow UX.
